SCOAS Execution Report - github

Timestamp: 2026-01-29-11-30

Summary

4117
Rule Violations
4.27
Average Violations/Route
3
Routes Without Violations
965
Routes Analyzed
2539
Responses Analyzed
If your OpenAPI Specification file contains status code misuses, they should be fixed based on the rule descriptions that are provided. The rule sources provide references to the standards (RFCs) that were used to define the rules.

Details

  • 858 violations of the rule has-400-if-params
  • 727 violations of the rule has-406-if-accept
  • 674 violations of the rule has-422-if-params
  • 454 violations of the rule has-404-if-path
  • 301 violations of the rule has-415-if-content-type
  • 300 violations of the rule has-413-if-content-length
  • 283 violations of the rule has-400-if-payload
  • 151 violations of the rule no-403-if-no-401
  • 148 violations of the rule has-422-if-payload
  • 147 violations of the rule no-401-if-no-auth
  • 28 violations of the rule has-204-if-no-content
  • 22 violations of the rule has-200-if-get
  • 14 violations of the rule has-200-or-201-or-204-if-post
  • 3 violations of the rule has-200-or-201-or-204-if-put
  • 3 violations of the rule has-200-or-204-if-delete
  • 2 violations of the rule no-201-if-patch
  • 1 violations of the rule has-200-or-204-if-patch
  • 1 violations of the rule no-201-if-get

Violation #1

Route: GET /

Responses: 200 OK

Parameters: /

Rule ID: has-406-if-accept

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 406 Not Acceptable in case the sever does not support the Accept header specified in the request. Only applies to routes that respond with content.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Route: GET /advisories

Responses: 200 OK, 429 Too Many Requests, 422 Unprocessable Content

Rule ID: has-400-if-params

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Responses: 200 OK, 422 Unprocessable Content, 404 Not Found, 403 Forbidden

Parameters: sha (path)

Rule ID: no-403-if-no-401

Rule Description: Never implement a response with the status code 403 Forbidden if the method does not implement a response with the status code 401 Unauthorized.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.4 (403 Forbidden) | RFC 9110, Section 15.5.2 (401 Unauthorized)

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 200 OK or 204 No Content in a PATCH method.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.3.1 (200 OK) | RFC 9110, Section 15.3.5 (204 No Content) | RFC 5789 (PATCH Method for HTTP)

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Responses: 201 Created, 202 Accepted, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found, 503 Service Unavailable

Parameters: /

Rule ID: no-401-if-no-auth

Rule Description: Never implement a response with the status code 401 Unauthorized if the specification does not contains an authentication mechanism.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.2 (401 Unauthorized)

Responses: 200 OK, 500 Internal Server Error, 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, 404 Not Found

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 406 Not Acceptable in case the sever does not support the Accept header specified in the request. Only applies to routes that respond with content.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.7 (406 Not Acceptable) | RFC 9110, Section 12.5.1 (Accept)

Responses: 200 OK, 301 Moved Permanently, 404 Not Found, 410 Gone, 422 Unprocessable Content

Parameters: /

Rule ID: has-415-if-content-type

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 415 Unsupported Media Type in case the server does not support the Content-Type header specified in the request.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.16 (415 Unsupported Media Type) | RFC 9110, Section 8.3 (Content-Type)

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.14 (413 Content Too Large) | RFC 9110, Section 8.6 (Content-Length)

Violation #3488

Route: PATCH /teams/{team_id}

Responses: 200 OK, 201 Created, 404 Not Found, 422 Unprocessable Content, 403 Forbidden

Parameters: /

Rule ID: has-415-if-content-type

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 415 Unsupported Media Type in case the server does not support the Content-Type header specified in the request.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.16 (415 Unsupported Media Type) | RFC 9110, Section 8.3 (Content-Type)

Violation #3489

Route: PATCH /teams/{team_id}

Responses: 200 OK, 201 Created, 404 Not Found, 422 Unprocessable Content, 403 Forbidden

Parameters: /

Rule ID: no-201-if-patch

Rule Description: Never implement a response with the status code 201 Created in a PATCH method (as it can never create data).

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.3.2 (201 Created) | RFC 5789 (PATCH Method for HTTP)

Violation #3490

Route: PATCH /teams/{team_id}

Responses: 200 OK, 201 Created, 404 Not Found, 422 Unprocessable Content, 403 Forbidden

Parameters: /

Rule ID: no-403-if-no-401

Rule Description: Never implement a response with the status code 403 Forbidden if the method does not implement a response with the status code 401 Unauthorized.

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.4 (403 Forbidden) | RFC 9110, Section 15.5.2 (401 Unauthorized)

Violation #3491

Route: DELETE /teams/{team_id}

Responses: 204 No Content, 404 Not Found, 422 Unprocessable Content

Parameters: /

Rule ID: has-400-if-params

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 400 Bad Request if the method contains parameters (in case of invalid syntax).

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.1 (400 Bad Request)

Responses: 200 OK

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.21 (422 Unprocessable Content)

Rule ID: has-422-if-params

Rule Description: Always implement a response with the status code 422 Unprocessable Content if the method contains parameters (in case of invalid semantics).

Rule Sources: RFC 9110, Section 15.5.21 (422 Unprocessable Content)
